name: marketing-strategy-pmm
description: Product marketing, positioning, GTM strategy, and competitive intelligence. Includes ICP definition, April Dunford positioning methodology, launch playbooks, competitive battlecards, and international market entry guides. Use when developing positioning, planning product launches, creating messaging, analyzing competitors, entering new markets, enabling sales, or when user mentions product marketing, positioning, GTM, go-to-market, competitive analysis, market entry, or sales enablement.
capabilities: Define Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) using firmographic, technographic, and psychographic criteria
Apply April Dunford positioning methodology to create differentiated value propositions
Build competitive battlecards with win/loss talk tracks for key competitors
Plan 90-day go-to-market launches with channel strategies and budget allocation
Conduct win/loss analysis to identify competitive trends and messaging improvements
Create international market entry plans for US, UK, DACH, France, and Canada
author: davila7
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidance and strategic frameworks. No executable code, file access, network calls, or system modifications. Static findings are false positives from misapplied security rules on markdown content.
valueStatement: Product marketing teams struggle to create consistent positioning, launch strategies, and competitive battlecards that actually win deals. This skill provides proven frameworks for ICP definition, positioning development, competitive intelligence, and go-to-market execution tailored for Series A+ B2B SaaS companies.
limitations: Does not execute marketing campaigns or access CRM systems directly
Templates require manual customization for specific company contexts
Does not provide real-time competitive intelligence data
Framework guidance assumes users have basic marketing knowledge

useCases.title: Create positioning and messaging
useCases.description: Develop differentiated positioning statements, messaging hierarchies, and persona-specific content for products and launches.

useCases.title: Plan quarterly GTM strategy
useCases.description: Design go-to-market plans, allocate budget across channels, and set launch timelines for product releases.

useCases.title: Build competitive battlecards
useCases.description: Create battlecards with objection handling, talk tracks, and proof points to help sales teams win more deals.

promptTemplates.title: Define ICP
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me define our Ideal Customer Profile for a [product type] targeting [market segment]. Include firmographics, technographics, and psychographic criteria.

promptTemplates.title: Position Product
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply the April Dunford positioning methodology to position our [product] against competitors including [competitor 1] and [competitor 2].

promptTemplates.title: Create Battlecard
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a competitive battlecard for [competitor] that highlights our strengths, addresses their key claims, and provides talk tracks for common objections.

promptTemplates.title: Plan Launch
promptTemplates.prompt: Develop a 90-day GTM launch plan for our new [product/feature] targeting [ICP] including pre-launch, launch, and post-launch phases.

bestPractices: Start positioning work with customer interviews to understand real pain points and language
Validate messaging with sales team before full rollout to catch gaps early
Update competitive battlecards monthly based on win/loss analysis and competitor changes
antiPatterns: Creating positioning without customer research or using internal jargon
Building generic battlecards without specific proof points or customer evidence
Launching without clear ICP definition or metrics for measuring success

faq.question: What company stages is this skill best suited for?
faq.answer: Designed for Series A+ B2B SaaS companies (20-5000 employees) with hybrid PLG and sales-led motions.

faq.question: Does this skill integrate with specific tools like HubSpot or Salesforce?
faq.answer: Provides frameworks and templates compatible with any CRM. Users apply concepts manually to their specific tools.

faq.question: How long does it take to develop a complete positioning strategy?
faq.answer: The framework spans 6 weeks: research, positioning, messaging, validation, and rollout phases.

faq.question: Is customer data handled securely?
faq.answer: This skill contains only guidance. No customer data is collected, stored, or transmitted by the skill itself.

faq.question: Why is win/loss analysis important for competitive strategy?
faq.answer: Win/loss interviews reveal why customers choose you or competitors, identifying actionable insights for positioning and battlecards.

faq.question: How does this differ from general marketing strategy skills?
faq.answer: Focuses specifically on B2B SaaS product marketing including positioning methodologies, competitive intelligence, and launch execution.
